As artificial intelligence pushes semiconductors and data centers to new physical limits, advances in materials science are becoming essential to sustaining the pace of innovation.

stories.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
stories.summary: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, t
stories.extract:
All the big entertainment apps are starting to look the same, and that’s not an accident. For a decade, platforms fought over who would dominate a single format: music, video, podcasts, audiobooks. Now, powered by AI, they’re fighting over something bigger — becoming the app you default to whenever you have time to kill, no matter what form the content takes.
There are several reasons why this is the case. The market for entertainment apps is reaching maturity, so growth has slowed, pushing comp

stories.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
stories.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.
stories.extract:
On Tuesday, Google DeepMind released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber. Gemini 3.6 Flash is Google’s “workhorse model” that promises improved capabilities in coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance while reducing token usage by up to 17%, making it cheaper than its predecessor 3.5 Flash.
Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite is the most cost-effective model in the class, and 3.5 Flash Cyber is a specialized model that was fine-tuned for finding and fixing cybersecurity vulnerabili

stories.title: Engineering and Governing the Agent Harness: A Technology and Policy Framework for the Runtime Layer of Agentic AI
stories.extract: The analysis draws on recent research on autonomous agents, tool use, agent evaluation and agent safety, together with public technical documentation, engineering essays, open-source code inspection and available analyses of closed-source systems. It compares contemporary implementations across coding agents, general-purpose assistants, orchestration libraries and research harnesses. The purpose is to clarify the harness as a concrete runtime object that can be documented, evaluated, procured an

stories.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
stories.summary: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.
stories.extract:
Music streaming company Deezer has been tracking the number of AI-generated tracks uploaded on the platform since last year, and the number has constantly gone up. Today, the company said that AI music now represents more than 50% of downloads.
Deezer said that AI-generated track uploads were at a peak in June 2026, representing a monthly average of 90,000 tracks per day.
The rapid rise of AI-generated music has forced streaming services to decide how much of it they want on their own platforms.

Funding the future: A student-driven AI solution
Per Scholas is a national nonprofit that provides no-cost, rigorous training to help adults launch high-growth tech careers. Because the organization's offerings are at no cost to the learners, Per Scholas relies on external funding sources to fuel its growth. Finding that funding has always been a taxing, time-consuming endeavor. Today, when money is particularly tight, fundraising efficiency is critical.

stories.extract:
Telefónica, through its business unit Telefónica Tech, has partnered with the global healthcare technology company Harrison.ai to offer an artificial intelligence-based solution in Spain that will support healthcare professionals during the diagnostic process for chest X-rays.
The technological solution incorporates a diagnostic support algorithm capable of analysing chest X-rays, automatically identifying up to 124 clinical findings (including pulmonary nodules to facilitate lung cancer screeni

stories.extract:
News directly from Cornell's colleges and centers
Researchers gather at Cornell Tech to explore AI for molecular science
By Kathryn Henion
As artificial intelligence becomes an increasingly powerful tool for discovering new molecules and materials, researchers are working to understand how – and in which cases – machine learning models should remain grounded in the laws of physics that govern the natural world.
That challenge was the focus of the CECAM Workshop on Physics-Aware Machine Learning

stories.title: Headaches for Silicon Valley as China chips away at the US’s lead in the AI race
stories.summary: Google’s AI struggles scream trouble as new Chinese models (again) throw US tech dominance into question Hello, I’m Blake Montgomery, writing to you after a double-header feature of Christopher Nolan’
stories.extract:
Hello, I’m Blake Montgomery, writing to you after a double-header feature of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey and the World Cup final. What a great Sunday. Today in tech, we’re discussing how China is chipping away at the US’s lead in the AI race and how Silicon Valley’s workers are taking action to protect their jobs from AI.
As Google falters on AI, China eats into the US’s lead
 Google is struggling with the latest version of its Gemini model

stories.title: How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table
stories.summary: Tech workers are increasingly unionizing, trading Silicon Valley’s myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining to contest the corporate deployment of artificial intelligence For decades, the tech
stories.extract:
For decades, the technology industry was a fortress that labor unions couldn’t breach. Tech workers already had cushy compensation packages, dream benefits like unlimited vacation and free lunch, and a flat corporate hierarchy that made engineers feel as powerful as their bosses, all of whom dressed down in sneakers and hoodies. So why unionize?
Now, that fortress is cracking from the inside. Unions have become increasingly popular for tech employees. After months of mass layoffs tied to artific

stories.title: Banks Wrap Up Loan Backing SoftBank’s $5.4 Billion Robotics Bet
stories.extract:
Banks Wrap Up Loan Backing SoftBank’s $5.4 Billion Robotics Bet
A group of banks wrapped up a loan offering backing SoftBank Group Corp.’s $5.4 billion robotics bet after increasing the size of the US dollar tranche.
The $1.75 billion-equivalent debt package, comprising dollar and euro term loans, priced at a margin of 4.25 percentage points over the respective benchmarks, at the wide end of initial talk, according to a person familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says
stories.summary: Industry says government’s failure to address cooling demands means AI growth plans are ‘fatally flawed’ The UK will not have enough water for future datacentres, the water industry has said in stark
stories.extract:
The UK will not have enough water for future datacentres, the water industry has said in stark criticism of the government’s AI growth plans.
Datacentres rely on large amounts of water to manage the heat generated by densely packed servers. Cooling towers, chillers and humidification systems make direct use of this water, with large volumes also consumed indirectly through high electrical power needs.
Water UK, the trade body that represents water companies in Britain and Northern Ireland, said

stories.title: Quantum adiabatic transport in a quantum anomalous Hall insulator
stories.extract:
Abstract
An exceptional trait of the quantum Hall effect is quantum adiabatic transport, dissipationless quantized edge transport that is robust to bias voltages multiple orders of magnitude larger than any known relevant energy scale. This enables stable and highly sensitive measurements in quantum metrology at temperatures above 1 K. In contrast, prior experiments have shown that an electrical bias of the same order applied to the quantum anomalous Hall edge modes in magnetic topological insul

stories.title: Industry Insights: Can Walden Robotics Make People the Center of Physical AI?
Why AI Needs a “Genie Coefficient”
stories.summary: Major benchmarks measure what AI can do. None measure whether it does what you mean: the distance between what you ask an AI to do and the unspoken assumptions about how you want the AI to do it. We p
stories.extract:
Major benchmarks measure what AI can do. None measure whether it does what you mean: the distance between what you ask an AI to do and the unspoken assumptions about how you want the AI to do it. We propose a new metric: the Genie coefficient.
There’s often a gap between one person’s request and another’s understanding. Most of the time, we bridge it using general knowledge. For example, if you ask a friend to get you coffee, they’ll pour a cup from the pot or buy one from a coffee shop. They wo

stories.title: Samsung Electronics shares rise as robotics move highlights push into physical AI
stories.extract:
Samsung Electronics shares rose after the company said Tuesday it is creating a robotics division as the South Korean tech giant seeks new sources of growth.
The newly created RX, or Robotics eXperience, will consolidate Samsung's robotics capabilities and is intended to drive a mid- to long-term strategy from core tech development to commercialization, the company said in an emailed statement. CEO TM Roh will oversee the division directly.
Samsung rose 6.76%, while the benchmark Kospi index was

stories.title: India's renewable energy installed capacity reaches 288.58 GW from 76.38 GW in 2014: Naik
stories.extract:
- Renewable
- 1 min read
India's renewable energy installed capacity reaches 288.58 GW from 76.38 GW in 2014: Naik
                    Renewable energy capacity grew from 76.38 GW in 2014 to 288.58 GW by June 2026. This expansion includes significant contributions from solar and wind power sources. Foreign direct investment reached $45.72 billion in the renewable energy sector.

stories.title: Colleges across India to come together to create Campus Biodiversity Registers
stories.extract:
In an attempt to record the diverse biodiversity within campuses, college and university campuses across India are coming together to discover, document and celebrate the biodiversity found on their campuses by creating Campus Biodiversity Registers (CBRs). The initiative is anchored by the Nature Conservation Foundation (NCF), together with the National Biodiversity Authority, Government of India (NBA).
As part of the initiative, the participants will record the plants, animals, fungi and other

stories.title: Millions face long-term coastal flooding risk in UK without climate action, scientists warn
stories.summary: A ‘reasonable worst-case scenario’ suggests need for large-scale movement of populations away from reshaped coast Up to 13 million people in the UK face the long-term risk of coastal flooding due to t
stories.extract:
Up to 13 million people in the UK face the long-term risk of coastal flooding due to the climate crisis, scientists have warned, with a “reasonable worst-case scenario” suggesting the need for the large-scale movement of populations away from a dramatically reshaped coast.
Even if today’s global pledges to cut carbon pollution are met, about 1.4 million more people will be exposed to coastal floods in 2200 and 1.7 million in 2300 due to the sea level rise driven by melting ice caps and warming o

stories.title: Tick bites are on the rise in the US and UK. Here’s how to protect yourself
stories.summary: Experts share tips to avoid the blood-sucking pests that can transmit Lyme disease and other illnesses on to humans I won’t go into gory detail about the tick I found latched behind my ear approximate
stories.extract:
I won’t go into gory detail about the tick I found latched behind my ear approximately 35 hours after a hike in early June. Suffice it to say, the parasite was well fed, and I was horrified.
I am far from alone in making such a disgusting discovery. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), emergency room visits for tick bites have been unusually high in the US this year. Thirty-one million Americans are bitten by ticks annually – and the number has been trending up for

stories.title: Stand still, make eye contact: Denmark tells tourists how to behave in a wolf encounter
stories.summary: Government urges visitors not to approach or feed wolves as it manages safety amid growing population Danish authorities have advised tourists to stand still, make eye contact and put up “maximum resi
stories.extract:
Danish authorities have advised tourists to stand still, make eye contact and put up “maximum resistance” in the event of attack if they encounter a wolf while on holiday.
After a 200-year absence, wolves started returning to Denmark in 2012. There are now estimated to be 49 wolves on the Danish peninsula of Jutland.
Generally, it is rare for wolves to even approach humans, but in the area around the popular tourism destination of Oksbøl, young ones were spotted doing so earlier this year.

stories.extract:
India's green hydrogen ambitions are no longer just policy announcements. Steel companies are piloting hydrogen-based production, ports are preparing clean fuel infrastructure, and industries are actively looking for low-carbon alternatives.
But behind every green hydrogen project lies one critical question:
Who will build the electrolysers and execute these projects?
Greenzo Energy believes it has the answer.

stories.title: STAT+: Makers of rare disease drugs seek exemption from Trump’s price-cut pilots
stories.summary: A coalition of biotech firms is lobbying to exclude orphan drugs from new federal pilot programs aimed at lowering Medicare drug costs.
stories.extract:
WASHINGTON — Biotech companies are lobbying the Trump administration to exclude treatments for rare diseases from programs that lower brand drug prices in Medicare.
The Rare Disease Company Coalition met last week with the White House Office of Management and Budget to discuss two pilot programs that are part of President Trump’s plan to get drugmakers to lower prices in the United States to levels charged in other rich countries, a policy generally referred to as most-favored nation.

Trump administration says it’s deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
stories.summary: The Trump administration said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance.”
stories.extract:
The Trump administration on Tuesday said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance,” the latest in a series of punitive steps it has linked to allegations of fraud in mostly Democratic-led states.
Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said the new actions — which come after previously announced Medicaid funding deferrals in those states — are part of the administration’s strategy to “stop the fraud before

stories.title: Rockefeller Foundation commits $4m to rebuild the rural economy through better farmland
stories.summary: Asset manager Alder Point's strategy treats land stewardship as both an environmental and economic opportunity. The post Rockefeller Foundation commits $4m to rebuild the rural economy through better
stories.extract:
Rebuilding farmland and rebuilding the rural economy are “inseparable objectives,” according to The Rockefeller Foundation, which just committed $4 million to real assets manager Alder Point Capital.
With the new investment, Alder Point, which facilitates investments in US farmland and timberland, will acquire and bring more under-managed land into responsible stewardship, boosting both its environmental performance and positive financial impact on surrounding communities.

BMW Group India has launched its annual Monsoon Service Campaign across its authorised dealer network to help customers prepare their vehicles for the challenges of the rainy season. The initiative is designed to provide preventive maintenance, vehicle inspections and aftersales support as changing weather conditions and heavy rainfall place additional demands on vehicles.

stories.extract:
You can read about day one of my India trip here, day two here, and day three here.
Today was a travel day from New Delhi to Mumbai, though timed in such a way as to minimize stress and maximize productivity.
When in the USA, I tend to take redeyes when flying east and reverse-redeye when available when flying west in order to maximize my time on the ground. But if time were not stretched so thin, I prefer afternoon flights. That removes the stress of getting up early. Even though I would call m

stories.title: Amazon tree rings reveal more intense drought and rainfall in the rainforest
stories.summary: Tree rings from the Amazon show that the world’s largest rainforest has developed wetter wet seasons and drier dry seasons over the past four decades. The findings point to intense seasonal swings tha
stories.extract:
Tree rings from the Amazon show that the world’s largest rainforest has developed wetter wet seasons and drier dry seasons over the past four decades. The findings point to intense seasonal swings that can cause significant harm to biodiversity and suggest that widespread drying out across the entire Amazon basin is unlikely.
Researchers collected tree cores from two species of floodplain trees; one species only grows in the wet season and another that only grows in dry conditions. They analyzed

stories.title: ‘We couldn’t breathe’: DRC villagers revive protest against Perenco over pollution

stories.extract:
- Residents of Kitombe, in southwestern Democratic Republic of Congo, accuse multinational oil giant Perenco, controlled by the French Perrodo family, of causing severe air and soil pollution linked to oil extraction activities.
- Villagers reported respiratory problems, dizziness, diarrhea and crop failures after a major gas release from a crude oil storage facility in April 2025.

- 
Farmers are gathering at Kisan Ghat in Delhi to protest the proposed US-India trade deal under the 'Desh Bachao Morcha' banner 
- 
They argue that reducing import duties on US agricultural products will create unfair competition for small Indian farmers 
- 
The farmers also claim that the bilateral agreement's provisions on e-commerce and digital trade could negatively impact the broader Indian public

stories.title: Humanoid Raises $152 Million At $1.35 Billion Valuation: Europe’s Newest Robot Unicorn
stories.extract: Europe has a new billion-dollar startup, and it’s Humanoid, a two-year-old company headquartered in London that developed its humanoid robot, HMND 01, in near-record time. Today, London-based Humanoid announced that it has raised a $152 million Series A at a $1.35 billion post-money valuation. The funding comes just months after the company signed a 1,000-robot deal with Schaeffler and secured production capacity, backed by Bosch, that would enable the production of 100,000 units over the next f

stories.title: Italian startup ORiS raises funding for laser power-beaming technology
stories.extract:
WASHINGTON — Italian startup ORiS has raised 5 million euros ($5.7 million) to help it develop wireless power-beaming technology for satellites using lasers.
The company, based in Turin, Italy, announced July 21 that it raised 4.5 million euros in a pre-seed funding round led by Earlybird and co-led by Pitchdrive, with participation from Galaxia, Vento and Piemonte Next Fund. The company also received a grant worth 500,000 euros from an Italian regional program for startups.

stories.title: A startup building a map to help you make plans with friends has doubled its funding
stories.extract:
Finding a really good restaurant or bar takes effort.
Corner, a startup looking to make that process easier, has raised more capital to stake its claim in the map game.
Corner, which was founded in 2022, is a social-mapping app targeting Gen Z users who want to meet up in real life. In the app, users can leave reviews, share photos, and curate lists. Locations are added to Corner's map only when a user saves it.
The platform has more than 250,000 users — primarily in New York and Europe — across

stories.extract:
US-based Sofab Inks has secured $6 million in a seed funding round led by Cloudberry Ventures, as the company looks to accelerate commercialization of its specialty materials aimed at improving the durability and scalability of perovskite solar technology.
The funding comes alongside the company's announcement of a manufacturing reliability breakthrough, addressing one of the key challenges hindering large-scale deployment of perovskite solar cells—long-term operational stability.

Automation, robotics, and sustainable materials startup Hyperion Robotics announced that it has raised $7.4 million in a growth funding round, with proceeds from the financing aimed at supporting the expansion of its platform to produce infrastructure components faster, at lower cost, and with a substantially lower carbon footprint.
Founded in 2020 by Fernando De los Rios, Ashish Mohite, and Henry Unte

India’s space startup drive takes off
Vikram-1 rocket demonstrates Skyroot Aerospace’s orbital launch capability
INDIA’s private space sector has entered an exciting phase with Skyroot Aerospace’s launch of Mission Aagaman, the maiden flight of its Vikram-1 rocket. Built, designed and tested by the Hyderabad-based startup with infrastructure support from the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), the rocket placed multiple payloads into low-Earth orbit.

stories.extract:
Daniel Docter, managing director at Dell Technologies Capital, began his career as a technologist. He holds degrees in electrical engineering and computer science, as well as a Ph.D., but early on found himself gravitating away from purely technical work toward translating technology into business and commercial use cases.
Docter also proved adept at securing funding for research and other projects, a skill that ultimately caught the attention of venture capital firms and led him into the indust

stories.title: Profitable space startup deltaVision secures €10.2 million to provide the “cardiovascular system for spacecraft”
stories.extract:
deltaVision, a Munich-based manufacturer of fluidic systems for launchers, satellites, lunar landers and orbital servicing vehicles, has raised €10.2 million in its first funding round to expand production to keep pace with growing demand.
The company secured this funding from both private industrial family offices and venture capital investors. KT Ventures and Valemount Capital led the round, both doubling down on their commitment after an earlier convertible loan. Futury Capital also participa

stories.title: Exclusive: Agriculture drone startup Suind set to raise Rs 20.5 Cr at Rs 80 Cr valuation
stories.extract:
Agriculture drone startup Suind is set to raise Rs 20.5 crore (around $2.2 million) in a fresh funding round led by Transition Venture Capital Fund, according to regulatory filings reviewed by Entrackr.
The board of the Bengaluru-based company has approved the issuance of 5,107 compulsorily convertible preference shares (CCPS) at an issue price of Rs 40,132.20 each to raise the capital.
As part of the round, Transition Venture Capital Fund will invest Rs 20 crore, while CIIE Initiatives will inf

stories.title: Indonesia Seen Hiking Rate in Close Call, Putting Rupiah Ahead of Slowing Growth
stories.summary: A narrow majority of economists expect Bank Indonesia to raise interest rates again to support the rupiah and contain inflation, even as the economy slows.
stories.extract:
Central Banks
Indonesia Seen Hiking Rate in Close Call to Help Fragile Rupiah
A narrow majority of economists expect Bank Indonesia to raise interest rates again to support the rupiah and contain inflation, even as the economy slows.
Twenty of 37 economists surveyed by Bloomberg expect Governor Perry Warjiyo and his board to deliver another quarter-point increase on Wednesday, which would lift the benchmark BI-Rate to 6%. The rest forecast no change.

stories.extract:
Hong Kong Home Prices Set to Rebound 19% Over Two Years, BI Says
Hong Kong home prices are expected to rebound the most in nearly a decade in 2026, driven by strong mainland Chinese demand, limited supply and robust rental growth, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
Secondary home prices in the city are set to rise a total of 19% over 2026 and 2027, BI estimated in a report. The projected 11% surge this year would mark the biggest gain in almost a decade, BI said. Robust housing demand from mai

stories.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
stories.summary: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy products Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country
stories.extract:
Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.
The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement.
The new t

stories.title: Social Darwinists to tech bros: tracing the long fight against equality in US
stories.summary: Historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s book Country of Lords looks back at the many men who have tried to fight against egalitarianism, from John Adams to Peter Thiel The historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s new book
stories.extract:
The historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s new book is published amid celebrations of the 250th anniversary of the declaration of independence and its truth “self-evident, that all men are created equal”. Country of Lords, however, is a history of Neo-Aristocrats, Social Darwinists, Tech Utopians, and the Long Fight Against Equality in America.
Concise and hard-hitting, the book arrives at a time of glaring social and economic inequality, Phillips-Fein seeing Elon Musk “emerging as a trillionaire very mu

stories.title: Trump Sets 100% Tariff on Generic Drugs With Two-Year Delay
stories.extract:
Trump Sets 100% Tariff on Generic Drugs With Two-Year Delay
Generic drug manufacturers will have two years to move production to the US or face a 100% import duty from August 2028, President Donald Trump said in a social media post Tuesday.
That levy would then double a year later, to 200%, in August 2029.

stories.title: Here’s Why Your Supply Chain Is Leaking Cash
stories.extract:
With $1.7 trillion tied up in inefficient supply chains, CFOs are making liquidity a core strategy.
There’s $1.7 trillion of working capital sitting on the balance sheets of the largest U.S. companies: not locked in failed investments or delayed acquisitions, but trapped in slow receivables, excess inventory, and payment structures designed for a different economic environment.
That money hasn’t disappeared. It remains tied up in processes that no longer reflect how companies manage risk, liquid

The new tariffs will roll out amid mounting warnings from economists and even some of his own advisers that a fresh trade war could unsettle financial markets, fuel inflation, and hurt American consumers ahead of midterm elections in

stories.extract:

The farmers later concluded their protest and announced their return after an assurance of their demands to be taken up with the Centre

stories.title: New trade pact a win-win for India and U.K.
stories.extract:
The India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which came into force on July 15, marks a pivotal moment in the evolution of bilateral ties. More than a conventional tariff-reduction pact, the agreement reflects a strategic alignment between two major democracies seeking to recalibrate their economic engagement in a rapidly shifting global order.
The India-U.K. CETA represents one of the most wide-ranging trade agreements concluded by India in recent years. Aligned with the bro

stories.title: RBA now twice as likely to hike interest rates as US-Iran war drives fuel prices higher

In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.”

stories.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
stories.summary: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in
stories.extract:
One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.
There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.
Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025, with 7.8% of people affected, down from 8.1% in 202

Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
- Published
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has dismissed the country's army commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi.
It comes just days after the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov - a move that triggered street protests across the country.
Initial rumours that the end of Fedorov's tenure was related to a conflict between him and Syrskyi were soon confirmed.

stories.title: Chelsea Wolfe Reveals New Album The Dark, Lines Up 2026 Tour
stories.summary: The LP’s lead single, “Cold,” is out now
stories.extract:
Chelsea Wolfe has announced her next album, The Dark, which is set to land on Loma Vista on August 21. The record’s lead single, “Cold,” is out now, along with hauntingly beautiful visuals placing Wolfe in the foggy wilderness.
Wolfe’s follow-up to 2024’s She Reaches Out to She Reaches Out to She was co-written with Jennifer Decilveo and Ben Chisholm, and includes contributions from Robin Finck, Justin Meldal-Johnsen, and Matt Chamberlain.
Starting in September and running through December, Wolf

stories.title: Greg Freeman Readies New Album and Tour
stories.summary: “Indu” leads the Vermont singer’s John Congelton-produced All Set the Bone
stories.extract:
Greg Freeman is prepping a swift follow-up to last year’s Burnover LP. Assisted by super-producer John Congleton, the Vermont indie-rock singer-songwriter will release All Set the Bone on October 2 via Transgressive/Canvasback. He is also hitting the road for a string of dates in the United States and Europe. Check out the itinerary—including support from Wendy Eisenberg and Robber Robber at some dates—below, along with a video of hand-drawn animations for new song “Indu.”
Freeman, who plays Bro

stories.title: How to unlock unlimited geothermal energy, anywhere we want
stories.summary: Earth contains more than enough heat to power humanity many times over, but so far only a few lucky places have been able to exploit it. Now we may have a way to tap this vast clean energy source almo
stories.extract:
Deep under your feet, Earth is hot. Really hot. Some of that heat is left over from the planet’s violent formation. More is continually created by the splitting of radioactive atoms in the rocks below. In a few places, Earth’s hidden furnace dramatically makes itself known, bursting through the crust as volcanoes, geysers and rivers of magma.
Spectacular stuff, but also extremely useful – if we can get at it. Conventional geothermal power works best wherever geology has done the hard work for us

stories.title: Health fears linger in a Brazilian neighborhood built over oil waste
stories.summary: SÃO SEBASTIÃO, Brazil — In front of the house where she has lived for decades, Cosma da Silva points to nearby homes in her Itatinga neighborhood and lists a string of cancer cases. “There’s a woman w
stories.extract:
- Official documents show the historic presence of benzene and other contaminants in São Sebastião’s Itatinga neighborhood. Despite reports of illnesses, an epidemiological study planned for assessing the impacts on the population has never been conducted.
- Documents from multiple agencies show the historic presence of oil-derived contaminants, including benzene, in a residential area of the Itatinga neighborhood in São Sebastião, São Paulo.

stories.title: Neuroscience findings often can't be replicated — and it's a big problem for what we know about the brain
stories.extract:
Neuroscience findings often can't be replicated — and it's a big problem for what we know about the brain
The findings in several foundational brain imaging studies don't hold up in follow-up research.
One of the central assumptions of modern neuroscience research is that the brain's shape and structure affect behavior. Scientists have linked a thicker cortex (the brain's outer layer) to higher intelligence, certain brain wave patterns to better volleyball ability, and higher connectivity betwee

stories.title: Amazon tree rings reveal more intense drought and rainfall in the rainforest
stories.summary: Tree rings from the Amazon show that the world’s largest rainforest has developed wetter wet seasons and drier dry seasons over the past four decades. The findings point to intense seasonal swings tha
stories.extract:
Tree rings from the Amazon show that the world’s largest rainforest has developed wetter wet seasons and drier dry seasons over the past four decades. The findings point to intense seasonal swings that can cause significant harm to biodiversity and suggest that widespread drying out across the entire Amazon basin is unlikely.
Researchers collected tree cores from two species of floodplain trees; one species only grows in the wet season and another that only grows in dry conditions. They analyzed

stories.title: ‘We couldn’t breathe’: DRC villagers revive protest against Perenco over pollution
stories.summary: “I had diarrhea and dizziness. Food no longer tasted the same. … I wasn’t the only one who noticed it — it was the same for everyone in the village; we couldn’t breathe,” Benoît Packera, secretary of
stories.extract:
- Residents of Kitombe, in southwestern Democratic Republic of Congo, accuse multinational oil giant Perenco, controlled by the French Perrodo family, of causing severe air and soil pollution linked to oil extraction activities.
- Villagers reported respiratory problems, dizziness, diarrhea and crop failures after a major gas release from a crude oil storage facility in April 2025.

stories.title: India’s Tour Of Shame
stories.extract:
Is BCCI Failing Indian cricket?
New Delhi, July 20: Indian cricket has sunk to a catastrophic nadir. The recent European tour was not just a failure; it was an absolute slaughter of the nation’s cricketing pride. A historic, stomach-turning 2-0 whitewash at the hands of Ireland, followed by a merciless 4-0 T20I thrashing and a 2-1 ODI series defeat by England, has exposed the toxic rot eating away at the national team.
At the heart of this sporting disaster sits a trifecta of accountability avoi

stories.title: How Four Indian Galleries Are Reshaping India’s Place at Art Basel
See the largest and most detailed map of universe’s magnetic fields
stories.extract:
Magnetic fields are a fundamental part of the universe. They govern how small particles – the building blocks of planets, stars, and ultimately galaxies – move through space.
We still don’t know how magnetic fields came to exist in the universe, but we do know they’re everywhere. Earth itself has a magnetic field that compasses and migrating birds respond to.
With radio telescopes, astronomers can use the light from distant galaxies to illuminate these otherwise invisible areas in space.
In our

In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.”

stories.extract:
The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has come in for heavy criticism from his predecessor, Sepp Blatter, and the La Liga president, Javier Tebas, with the pair united in the belief that he should stand down.
As Spain celebrated their World Cup win on Sunday, Tebas accused Infantino of “destroying the football industry” by prioritising the World Cup over national competitions. He claimed “his time is up”. Blatter has called for “fans, players and national associations to reclaim football and bri

New Delhi: Pakistan’s effort to rally international support for the restoration of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) received no visible endorsement from Canada Monday, after Foreign Minister Anita Anand declined to publicly address Islamabad’s appeal during a joint press conference in Islamabad.
